name: Elasticsearch
description: Distributed search and analytics engine. Index, search, and analyze documents across one or more indices via the Elasticsearch REST API.

credential_fields.name: token
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Encoded Elasticsearch API key (the base64 'encoded' value generated by Kibana > Stack Management > API Keys, or the POST /_security/api_key response field).

tools.name: search
tools.description: Run a search query against one or more indices using the Elasticsearch Query DSL. Returns matching documents with relevance scores, plus optional aggregations.

index.description: Index name or comma-separated list of indices to search (use '_all' or '*' for every index).

size.description: Maximum number of hits to return. Defaults to 10.

from.description: Starting offset for pagination. Defaults to 0.

aggs.description: Aggregations to compute over matching documents.

_source.description: Fields to include in each hit's _source. Pass false to exclude _source entirely.

tools.name: get_document
tools.description: Retrieve a single document by its _id from a specific index.

index.description: Name of the index containing the document.

tools.name: index_document
tools.description: Index (create or replace) a document. Provide an id to upsert a specific document, or omit it to let Elasticsearch auto-generate one (uses POST /_doc).

index.description: Target index name. Created automatically if it does not exist (subject to cluster settings).

id.description: Document _id. Required for PUT; for auto-generated ids use the bulk_index tool or omit and switch method to POST.

refresh.description: If 'true' the affected shards are refreshed so the change is visible to search immediately. Use 'wait_for' to block until the next refresh.

tools.name: delete_document
tools.description: Delete a document by its _id from a specific index.

index.description: Name of the index containing the document.

tools.name: bulk
tools.description: Perform multiple index, create, update, or delete operations in a single request using the Elasticsearch bulk API. The body must follow the newline-delimited JSON format described in Elasticsearch docs.

body.description: Newline-delimited JSON: each action line (e.g. { 'index': { '_index': 'foo', '_id': '1' } }) followed by its document, ending with a final newline.

tools.name: list_indices
tools.description: List all indices in the cluster with their health, document count, and size, via the _cat/indices API. Returns JSON when format=json is set.

format.description: Response format. Use 'json' for structured output.

v.description: Include column headers when format is the default plain text.

tools.name: create_index
tools.description: Create a new index, optionally specifying mappings, settings, and aliases.

index.description: Name of the index to create.

mappings.description: Field mappings for the index (e.g. { properties: { title: { type: 'text' } } }).

settings.description: Index-level settings such as number_of_shards and number_of_replicas.

aliases.description: Aliases to associate with the new index.

tools.name: delete_index
tools.description: Delete an index and all of its documents. This action is irreversible.

index.description: Name of the index to delete.

tools.name: count
tools.description: Return the number of documents matching a query (or all documents in the index if no query is provided).

index.description: Index name or comma-separated list of indices.

query.description: Optional Query DSL object to scope the count.

tools.name: cluster_health
tools.description: Return the cluster health status (green/yellow/red), node counts, and shard statistics.
